description:
## Background

Automatic refresh should be invisible when Beadwork and git are healthy. It must not, however, make the user lose the last good Issue List because a refresh probe or a Beadwork command briefly fails. The existing initial-load failure presentation is appropriate when no Issue List exists yet, but it is too disruptive after a successful snapshot is already visible.

ADR-0007 distinguishes transient failures from structural failures. This slice makes that distinction user-visible and consistent across automatic ref-change refreshes, while retaining the last known good Issue List.

## What to build

When automatic refresh encounters a temporary error, keep the visible Issue List unchanged and retry silently. After five consecutive failures of the same transient class, show a non-disruptive banner above the list explaining that automatic refresh is failing.

When the problem is structural — `git` missing from PATH, `bw` missing from PATH, or a Workspace that is not a Beadwork Workspace — show the same banner immediately. Never replace an already-rendered Issue List with the initial full-pane failure state solely because an automatic refresh failed.

## Technical Approach

- Add a refresh-specific error classifier at the backend boundary. It distinguishes ref-probe failures, Beadwork loader failures, missing `git`, missing `bw`, invalid Beadwork Workspace, and no-Current-Workspace idle state.
- Maintain failure counters per transient class. A successful operation for that class resets its counter; structural failures bypass the retry budget.
- Publish typed refresh failure events through the same refresh event contract as successful updates. Include workspace identity, workspace-selection generation, refresh revision, error kind, message, and whether the error is transient.
- Extend frontend refresh state with non-blocking banner presentation. The banner is admitted only for the active Workspace and refresh generation; the last successful `IssueExplorerLoadState` remains rendered below it.
- Preserve the existing full-pane error behavior for a first load that has no last good snapshot.

## Considerations

- The ref probe and the three Beadwork view loaders can fail independently. A successful SHA probe must not erase a pending loader-failure count, and a successful loader must not hide a still-failing probe.
- Missing `git` is new user-facing dependency behavior introduced by ADR-0007; the banner must state why git is needed.
- No Current Workspace is not an error. The refresh service is idle in that state and must not increment a counter or show a banner.
- The banner is for refresh health, not Workspace switching validation; do not merge it with unrelated workspace-selector errors.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] One through four consecutive transient ref-probe or loader failures leave the last good Issue List visible with no banner.
- [ ] Five consecutive failures of one transient class show a refresh banner while retaining the last good Issue List.
- [ ] A successful operation resets only its own transient-failure counter.
- [ ] Missing `git` and missing `bw` surface immediately with clear, distinct banner copy.
- [ ] A non-Beadwork Workspace surfaces immediately through the refresh banner after it was previously showing a last good snapshot.
- [ ] The empty-workspace state remains banner-free and does not accumulate failures.
- [ ] A stale failure event from a previous Workspace or refresh revision is ignored.

## Test Plan

- Unit-test classification and counter reset semantics for every failure class.
- Test transient failure events followed by recovery: no visible banner before the threshold, banner at the threshold, banner cleared after recovery.
- Test structural-error events: immediate banner with the correct message for missing git, missing bw, and invalid Workspace.
- Frontend tests assert the Issue List remains rendered under the banner and that stale failure events are rejected.
- Add a desktop-level proof that a temporary refresh failure does not replace an already visible Issue List.
title: Preserve the Issue List through automatic refresh failures
